channel.title: Chart Breakers & Star Stories
channel.itunes:author: Family TVR
channel.itunes:summary: Did your favorite artist just break a record or a heart? The charts are screaming, and we have the inside story you won't hear anywhere else.
channel.description: The latest music trends, chart updates, and star stories from Billboard and beyond. Stay updated with the Pop Culture.

item.title: Bruno Mars Kicks off The Romantic Tour in Las Vegas & Receives Key to the Strip on ‘Bruno Mars Day’
item.description:
[Intro music plays]
Host: Welcome to "Trending on Billboard". I'm your host, and today we're talking about the one and only Bruno Mars. The King of Las Vegas entertainment kicked off his highly anticipated Romantic Tour on Friday night at Allegiant Stadium in front of a sold-out crowd of over 60,000 fans.
[Music transitions to a funky, upbeat tune]
Host: Bruno Mars did not disappoint, delivering a 25-track setlist that included five songs with his Silk Sonic partner, Anderson .Paak. But the celebration didn't stop at the concert. The city of Las Vegas declared April 10th "Bruno Mars Day" and honored the singer with a parade down the Strip, a key to the city, and even renamed a street after him - Bruno Mars Drive.
[Music transitions to a brief sound bite of a crowd cheering]
Host: MGM Resorts International CEO, Bill Hornbuckle, praised Bruno Mars, saying he's become an integral part of Las Vegas, drawing hundreds of thousands of visitors and creating unforgettable moments. And it's not just about the performances - Bruno Mars is also giving back to the community. He's pledged a $1 million donation to the future Intermountain Health Nevada Children's Hospital.
[Music transitions back to the main theme]
Host: The Romantic Tour continues tonight at Allegiant Stadium, and we can't wait to see what's in store. Bruno Mars' album, "The Romantic", debuted at number one on the Billboard 200 chart, with 186,000 equivalent album units earned in its first week. It's shaping up to be a big year for this global superstar.
[Outro music plays]
Host: That's all for today. Thanks for tuning in to "Trending on Billboard". We'll be back with more music news and updates. Until next time, keep on trending!
